name: Don McLean
abstract: Donald McLean III (; born October 2, 1945) is an American singer-songwriter and guitarist. Known as the "American Troubadour" or "King of the Trail", he is best known for his 1971 hit "American Pie", an eight-and-a-half-minute folk rock song that has been referred to as a "cultural touchstone".

concerts.label: Don McLean, Singer-Songwriter, with Special Guests The Jordanaires

about_quotes.speaker: David Spinozza
about_quotes.evidence: he called me and Paul Griffin, the piano player, to be the two studio guys on the record... I say to Don McLean, “You know you really should cut out some verses.”
about_quotes.doc_title: David Spinozza interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Hartford, NY, September 27, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Joel Dorn
about_quotes.evidence: I started working with Asleep at the Wheel, Don McLean, Lou Rawls, Steve Goodman, Peter Allen, and I made what I think are some very interesting, very good records.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joel Dorn interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2007
